description:
Compliance audit – failed SSH log-in summary
  
  Background
  -----------
  You are a compliance officer who must audit yesterday’s SSH activity on an
  application host.  
  All raw logs have already been copied to  
  /home/user/audit_logs/ for you to process.  
  Your job is to analyse the single file
 
      /home/user/audit_logs/auth.log
 
  and produce a concise report that management tools can ingest.  
  The report must summarise the three source IP addresses that generated the
  most FAILED password attempts during the day.

  What you have to do
  -------------------
  1. Read /home/user/audit_logs/auth.log and identify every line that indicates
     an SSH password failure.  Those lines all contain the literal substring

         "Failed password"

     followed by the source address introduced by the keyword “from”.
  2. Count how many failed log-in lines belong to each unique source
     IP address.
  3. Identify the *three* IP addresses with the highest totals.  
     • If two IPs have identical totals, sort those IPs in ascending
       lexicographic order (i.e. 192.0.* comes before 198.51.* etc.).  
     • The final list itself must appear in descending order of the totals.
  4. For each of the selected IPs determine  
     • the time stamp (HH:MM:SS) of the first failure line for that IP  
     • the time stamp of the last failure line for that IP.
  5. Create the file

         /home/user/audit_logs/compliance_summary.csv

     with *exactly* the following CSV layout (no spaces around commas):

         ip_address,total_failed_logins,first_seen,last_seen
         <ip1>,<count1>,<first_time1>,<last_time1>
         <ip2>,<count2>,<first_time2>,<last_time2>
         <ip3>,<count3>,<first_time3>,<last_time3>

     • The header line must appear *exactly* as shown.  
     • Each data line must use the same column order as the header.  
     • Time stamps must be in 24-hour HH:MM:SS form and must come from the
       corresponding “Failed password” log entries.

  6. Save everything inside /home/user/audit_logs/; no files elsewhere.

  Verification notes
  ------------------
  • The automated grader will look only at the existence and exact textual
    contents of /home/user/audit_logs/compliance_summary.csv.  
  • Make absolutely sure you do **not** include extra blank lines or
    trailing spaces.  
  • The grader ignores the order of commands you used, but it expects
    the file contents to be byte-for-byte identical to the specification
    above.

  Hints (you may ignore)
  ----------------------
  • Tools such as grep, awk, sort, uniq, cut and tee can all be combined
    with pipes to accomplish this in a single compound command, but you
    may also script it step by step.  
  • Remember to quote any literal text containing spaces when grepping.
truth:
Initial data
  ------------
  File: /home/user/audit_logs/auth.log  
  Contents:

  Jan 10 10:01:02 server sshd[12345]: Failed password for invalid user admin from 203.0.113.10 port 54321 ssh2
  Jan 10 10:05:22 server sshd[12346]: Failed password for invalid user test from 198.51.100.42 port 54421 ssh2
  Jan 10 10:07:11 server sshd[12347]: Failed password for root from 203.0.113.10 port 54456 ssh2
  Jan 10 10:15:45 server sshd[12348]: Failed password for invalid user guest from 203.0.113.10 port 54671 ssh2
  Jan 10 11:20:13 server sshd[12349]: Failed password for invalid user admin from 192.0.2.77 port 54781 ssh2
  Jan 10 12:25:39 server sshd[12350]: Failed password for invalid user user from 198.51.100.42 port 54931 ssh2
  Jan 10 13:02:54 server sshd[12351]: Failed password for root from 198.51.100.42 port 55043 ssh2
  Jan 10 15:14:22 server sshd[12352]: Failed password for invalid user admin from 203.0.113.10 port 55211 ssh2
  Jan 10 16:55:03 server sshd[12353]: Failed password for invalid user admin from 198.51.100.42 port 55344 ssh2
  Jan 10 17:35:49 server sshd[12354]: Failed password for invalid user guest from 198.51.100.42 port 55433 ssh2
  Jan 10 18:42:05 server sshd[12355]: Failed password for root from 203.0.113.10 port 55510 ssh2
  Jan 10 19:12:37 server sshd[12356]: Failed password for invalid user noob from 192.0.2.77 port 55688 ssh2

  Expected output file
  --------------------
  Path: /home/user/audit_logs/compliance_summary.csv  
  Exact byte-for-byte contents:

  ip_address,total_failed_logins,first_seen,last_seen
  198.51.100.42,5,10:05:22,17:35:49
  203.0.113.10,5,10:01:02,18:42:05
  192.0.2.77,2,11:20:13,19:12:37

  (The file ends with a single newline character after the last line.)

  Directory /home/user/audit_logs/ is owned by the non-root user and is
  writable.
